title: Firewall Rule Change Blocking Critical API Traffic
symptoms: 502 Bad Gateway errors on all API endpoints served by Azure backend
API gateway unable to reach backend services on port 443
NSG flow logs showing DENY actions for API gateway source IPs
Backend services healthy and responsive when accessed directly
Only traffic from the expanded API gateway subnet range blocked
root_cause: Network Security Group rule update during security hardening did not include the expanded API gateway subnet range in the allow list, blocking legitimate traffic
resolution: Added the expanded API gateway subnet range to the NSG allow rules, verified traffic flow restoration, and implemented NSG change validation
preventive_actions: Implement pre-deployment NSG rule validation against known traffic patterns
Create automated testing for NSG changes using synthetic API traffic
Maintain a service dependency map linking NSG rules to traffic flows
Require dual-approval for NSG rule modifications in production
Add NSG flow log monitoring for unexpected DENY events

timeline.event: NSG rule change applied as part of security hardening initiative
Automated alert fires for API error rate spike
David Kim begins investigation, backend services confirmed healthy
NSG flow logs reveal DENY actions for API gateway subnet
Root cause identified as missing subnet range in new rules
NSG rules updated to include expanded API gateway subnet
Traffic flow restored, API endpoints operational

embedding_summary: Azure Network Security Group rule change during security hardening blocked API gateway traffic on port 443 for 18 minutes. The expanded API gateway subnet was not included in the updated allow rules. 4,200 API calls per minute failed. Resolved by adding the correct subnet range to NSG rules.
